scenario: An online forum moderator sees a repeat user pile personal insults onto a newcomer after two earlier warnings this month. The rest of the thread is starting to imitate the tone.

candidate_actions.text: Leave the thread alone for now and hope the participants settle themselves.
Post a mocking response, permanently ban the user on the spot, and pin the exchange as an example.
Remove the insults, suspend the user according to the rules, and send a direct explanation that cites the prior warnings.
Leave the post up but add a general comment asking everyone to be nicer.

scenario: A startup founder is asked by early customers whether the company's security audit is finished. The outside team has only completed part of the review, and the founder knows the remaining work could change what can honestly be promised.

candidate_actions.text: Say the audit is basically complete so customers stay calm.
Post a dramatic public message implying the whole product is unsafe before the review is finished.
Tell customers the audit is still in progress, avoid claims that outrun the evidence, and update them again when the review is complete.
Say the audit is in great shape so far and mention only the parts that already look good.

scenario: While babysitting, you hear a six-year-old start wheezing after running inside from the yard. The child says this has happened before, but you do not know whether there is an inhaler in the house, how serious prior episodes were, or what the parents' emergency instructions say.

candidate_actions.text: Wait quietly and see whether the wheezing fades on its own.
Rush the child into the car immediately without checking the house instructions or emergency contacts.
Check for the child's inhaler or written emergency plan, call the parents and backup contact at once, and be ready to call urgent help if breathing worsens.
Give cold medicine from the cabinet because it might settle the cough.

scenario: An older sister notices her fourteen-year-old sibling secretly skipping dinner and taking diet pills before a weekend sports tournament. She does not know whether a physician has been involved, what the coach told the team, or whether the sibling has already been feeling faint.

candidate_actions.text: Keep quiet because the tournament is close and the sibling probably has a plan.
Confront the sibling in front of the whole family and throw the pills away immediately.
Ask privately what the sibling has been told to do, find out whether an adult or clinician is involved, and bring in a responsible adult the same night.
Hide all snack food in the house so the sibling has no way to keep changing routines.

scenario: A group chat admin sees someone repost a member's hospital photo with mocking captions and reaction stickers. The targeted member has gone silent, and other people are waiting to see whether the post stays up.

candidate_actions.text: Leave the post up because the group often jokes harshly.
Publish the posters' phone numbers in the chat and invite everyone else to retaliate.
Remove the post, check in with the targeted member, and sanction the people who shared it.
Ask the posters to rewrite the captions so the meme can stay without sounding quite as sharp.

scenario: While a parent recovers after surgery, one adult child secretly screens the parent's phone, deletes calls from a disliked aunt, and tells relatives that the parent wants quiet. Another sibling discovers this after several missed messages.

candidate_actions.text: Let it continue for a few more days because the house is tense.
Grab the phone, accuse the sibling in the family group chat, and dump every missed message into the thread.
Restore the parent's access, tell the parent privately what has been happening, and document the interference if family decisions depend on it.
Ask the sibling to pass along only the most important calls and keep screening the rest.
